items.title: History of U.S. Foreign Policy (1890s-present)
items.source_text: This graduate-level seminar explores the history of U.S. foreign policy since the rise to imperialism at the end of the 19th century...pays particular attention to the building confrontation with the Islamic world that forms the backdrops to 9/11, the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq, and the crisis with Iran.

items.title: U.S. Diplomatic History—20th Century
topics: FRUS (Foreign Relations of the United States)
items.source_text: History 2340W explores American foreign policy during the 'long 20th-century' (i.e., 1890s to the present), using primary and secondary historical sources to examine key events, themes, and interpretations.

items.title: The U.S. and the Modern World
items.source_text: This readings course examines the history of the United States and its relations with the world from the turn of the 20th century to the present.

items.title: U.S. Foreign Relations Since 1914
items.source_text: History 451 will attempt to provide the student with a basic understanding of the growth and development of American foreign relations from 1914 to the present while emphasizing the complexity of causal relationships and encouraging creative and imaginative thought through classroom discussion of major historical controversies.

items.title: U.S. Foreign Relations to 1914
items.source_text: The instructor states the course will provide the student with a basic understanding of the growth and development of American foreign relations from the colonial origins to 1914 while analyzing specific incidents in early American diplomacy, emphasizing especially the complexity of causal relationships.

items.title: Rethinking Cold War History
items.source_text: This graduate-level seminar explores the history of the Cold War, the superpower rivalry between the United States and Soviet Union that dominated international relations for nearly half a century, from the close of World War II until the dissolution of the USSR in 1991.
